blocks.text: “Kurukukuuu! Wake up, Zingisile! Today is your birthday!”
it’s It’s also my birthday, a school and day.

pages.text: “Mama, Mama! Guess what day it is?” I shout. I always ask my mama every morning on my birthday.

blocks.text: “Mama, Mama! Guess what day it is?” I shout.
I always ask my mama every morning on my birthday.

pages.text: “This is your birthday porridge. Now, quickly, eat up and get ready. You can’t be late on your birthday!”

blocks.text: “This is your birthday porridge. Now, quickly, eat up and get ready.
You can’t be late on your birthday!”

pages.text: Mmmhhh. I love mealie it’s runny... porridge when Gulp! Gulp! Gulp!

blocks.text: Mmmhhh. I love mealie it’s runny... porridge when
Gulp!
Gulp! Gulp!

pages.text: At preschool everyone knows it’s my birthday. They sing happy birthday and give me handmade love notes.

blocks.text: At preschool everyone knows it’s my birthday.
They sing happy birthday and give me handmade love notes.

pages.text: I can’t wait for my special meal from Mama. I can’t wait to see my favourite cousin, Sisipho. She is also my best friend. Her birthday is coming in September. Which chicken will Mama cook for me? I hope it’s the fat one.

blocks.text: I can’t wait for my special meal from Mama.
I can’t wait to see my favourite cousin, Sisipho. She is also my best friend. Her birthday is coming in September.
Which chicken will Mama cook for me? I hope it’s the fat one.

pages.text: “Zingisile, what was your favourite part of the story?” asks Mrs Mkombe. “Pumpkin and mealie pap!” I shout, still in my daydream. All my friends laugh.

blocks.text: “Zingisile, what was your favourite part of the story?” asks Mrs Mkombe.
“Pumpkin and mealie pap!” I shout, still in my daydream. All my friends laugh.

pages.text: When I get home, Luhambo is excited. He has only had one birthday, on the 28th of April. Now I’ve had five birthdays already!

blocks.text: When I get home, Luhambo is excited. He has only had one birthday, on the 28th of April.
Now I’ve had five birthdays already!

pages.text: Look at all the food! Mama has made my favourites – spinach and sweetcorn and pumpkin and mealie pap and gravy and her special bread. Everything is from our garden.

blocks.text: Look at all the food! Mama has made my favourites – spinach and sweetcorn and pumpkin and mealie pap and gravy and her special bread. Everything is from our garden.

pages.text: “Happy birthday, Zingisile! Mini emnandi kuwe, Zingisile! How old are you now?”

blocks.text: “Happy birthday, Zingisile! Mini emnandi kuwe, Zingisile! How old are you now?”

pages.text: “I’m five years old now!” I sing back. My papa picks me up and spins me around. “My Zing-star! You’re so old you’re almost a teacher!”

blocks.text: “I’m five years old now!” I sing back. My papa picks me up and spins me around.
“My Zing-star! You’re so old you’re almost a teacher!”

pages.text: Mama lets me pick the biggest piece of chicken. She dishes for me first. I dip my delicious bread in the gravy and I smile. I can’t wait for my next birthday!

blocks.text: Mama lets me pick the biggest piece of chicken. She dishes for me first.
I dip my delicious bread in the gravy and I smile. I can’t wait for my next birthday!
